key_themes: Analyst upgrades (Deutsche Bank to Buy) and consensus Buy rating with ~$91-92 price targets
Strong 2026 profit guidance ($13-15B EBIT) driven by trucks/SUVs, buybacks, and dividend increases
Low valuation (~6x earnings) and recent stock momentum to ~$81
Near-term headwinds from Q1 sales decline (~10%), tariffs ($750M-$1B), and EV transition costs
Tightening inventory, stable demand signals, and improving EV losses
summary: Financial sentiment for GM is solidly positive. Analysts maintain a Buy consensus with average 12-month targets near $92 (implying ~13% upside from ~$81), citing undervaluation, robust 2026 guidance for up to $2B profit growth, $6B in buybacks, and higher dividends. Recent Deutsche Bank upgrade, sector strength, and tightening inventory support optimism despite Q1 U.S. sales declining 9.7% on tough comps/weather and tariff costs; Q1 earnings on April 28 will be pivotal.
sources: 20+ web news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, GM Authority, NYT), 10+ recent X posts discussing upgrades, stock moves, earnings outlook, and guidance
